member.name: Abtisam Mohamed
member.synopsis: Abtisam Mohamed is the Labour MP for Sheffield Central , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Greyhound Racing: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, whether she held discussions with the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, on commissioning independent research into the social cost of greyhound racing betting as part of
writtenQuestions.answerText: Greyhound racing makes an important contribution to British cultural life and the department’s priority is ensuring the continued growth of the sport.DCMS engages regularly with DEFRA regarding greyhound racing, but has had no such discussions.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Immigration: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential implications for her policies of data from the Migration Advisory Committee report entitled The Fiscal of Immigration, December 2025, which found the actu
writtenQuestions.answerText: The MAC’s December 2025 report concluded that the net fiscal contribution of migrant workers declines after settlement. Their report did not provide the estimated savings from implementing an earned settlement model.The consultation for the earned settlem...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sheffield Hallam University: Childcare
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, if she will ask Sheffield Hallam University to reconsider its proposal to close its Collegiate nursery provision.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I would like to say how sorry I was to read of the proposed closure of Sheffield Hallam’s nursery. I know how devastating such closures can be and the impact this can have on families and the wider community. Under Section 6 of the Childcare Act 2006, loc...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pre-school Education: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what progress she has made on the commitment to review early years funding, including national funding formulae, and consult the sector on changes by summer 2026; and when the consultation will launch.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We remain committed to engaging closely with stakeholders and launching the early years funding consultation in line with our stated timeframe of summer 2026. Further details on the date of publication will be confirmed in due course.

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office works closely with sponsors and sector bodies to support compliance with sponsorship duties and the integrity of the immigration system.The Home Office regularly shares information with sponsors, including data to support monitoring of vis...

writtenQuestions.heading: Visas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of the visa brake on applicants who have been long-term residents in third countries.
writtenQuestions.answerText: By year ending September 2025, asylum claims along the affected routes by nationals of Afghanistan, Cameroon, Myanmar, and Sudan had risen to over 470% of their 2021 level. The visa brake operates on a nationality-based approach on the Student visa route ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Greyhound Racing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, whether her Department collects data on (a) track closures, (b) number of races and (c) attendance for greyhound racing.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DCMS engages regularly with the governing body for greyhound racing, the Greyhound Board of Great Britain (GBGB), to keep abreast of developments in the sport, including those relating to tracks, races and attendance.Data on tracks and attendances are ava...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Immigration: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what discussions she has had with the Department for Work and Pensions and the Department for Education on the impact of earned settlement proposals on the Child Poverty Strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Proposals for introducing an earned settlement model, as set out in the Command Paper “A Fairer Pathway to Settlement” (CP1448), were subject to a public consultation, which opened on 20 November 2025 and closed on 12 February 2026.We are currently consid...

writtenQuestions.heading: Immigration: Children and Young People

writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is fully committed to the European Convention on Human Rights and our obligations under the UN Convention on the rights of the child. However, the government recognises the family and private life rules are not working as intended.As we wo...

registeredInterests.category: 3. Gifts, benefits and hospitality from UK sources

items.text: Name of donor: Sheffield City Council Address of donor: Howden House, 1 Union Street, Sheffield City Centre S1 2HH Amount of donation or nature and value if donation in kind: Two tickets (myself plus a guest) to a session of the final of World Snooker. No hospitality was included and I have donated the full value of the tickets to a local charity, value £452 Date received: 3 May 2026 Date accepted: 3 May 2026 Donor status: other (Local Authority) (Registered 28 May 2026)
